Integrated in silico and in vitro evaluation of Genistein and Apigenin as dual inhibitors of PARP1 and ESR1 in breast cancer

evidence_entities.text: Docking showed strong affinities of Apigenin for PARP1 (–9.6 kcal/mol) and GNT for ESR1 (–8.3 kcal/mol)
In vitro, GNT and Apigenin produced dose-dependent cytotoxicity against HCC1937 cells with IC50 values of 67.01 ± 1.67 µM and 70.24 ± 1.45 µM, respectively
KEGG enrichment highlighted pathways including estrogen signaling, endocrine resistance, and PI3K–Akt signaling
MD simulations and MM-GBSA confirmed stable binding with favorable free energy (ΔG_bind = − 74.9 kcal/mol)
